items: 2 garlic cloves, peeled and thinly sliced
40g unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
4 sea bass fillets (240g), skinned, sliced into 5cm pieces

steps: Bring a large pot of water to the boil, add salt and cook pasta for 1-2 minutes less than packet directions.
Once cooked, drain, and reserve 150ml of the pasta cooking water.
While pasta is cooking, heat a large, high-sided frying pan on a medium-high heat and toast the nori sheets for 15 seconds each side.
Once shiny and crisp, crush sheets into a bowl, reserving ⅓ of the crushed sheets for garnish.
With the pan still hot, add in two tablespoons of oil, half the capers, half the garlic and half the chilli.
Once sizzling, fry for two minutes until garlic just becomes golden, then remove and pour into a heatproof bowl.
Put in remaining two-thirds of crushed nori, 1 Tbsp EVOO, capers, garlic and chilli flakes, all the parsley, half the butter and a twist of black pepper.
Fry, stirring frequently for about a minute until fragrant but not coloured.
Add the sliced fish and sprinkle over salt and fry for two minutes, moving pan occasionally until fish is almost opaque.
Add in spaghetti, reserved pasta water, lemon juice and remaining butter, turn up the heat and vigorously toss for about two minutes.
Once sauce is silky and clinging to pasta, divide the pasta between two bowls, sprinkle over reserved nori and drizzle with reserved chilli garlic oil.

recipe_info.title: Spaghetti with Sea Bass and Nori Butter
recipe_info.intro: A (notionally) 30 minute meal created by Yotam Ottolenghi, can be doubled to make 4-5 serves.
